input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discover latest stable version first)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --silent

# Refresh PATH
$env:Path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path","Machine") + ";" + [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path","User")

# Install the MAUI workload
dotnet workload install maui
```

## 2. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target dependencies)

```powershell
# MAUI Windows apps need the Windows App SDK & WinUI. Easiest via VS workload:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --silent `
  --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.MSIX.Packaging `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
              --quiet --wait"
```

> If VS is already installed, replace `install` with `upgrade` or use the Visual Studio Installer UI.

## 3. Install Java JDK (Android build requirement)

```powershell
# MAUI Android needs JDK 17
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --silent

# Set JAVA_HOME (refresh after install)
$env:JAVA_HOME = (Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-*" | Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", $env:JAVA_HOME, "User")
```

## 4. Install Android SDK

```powershell
# Option A — let MAUI auto-provision (easiest):
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android `
  -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk" `
  -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true

# Option B — manual via command-line tools:
$androidHome = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidHome, "User")

# Download command-line tools
$clToolsUrl = "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-11076708_latest.zip"
Invoke-WebRequest $clToolsUrl -OutFile "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip"
Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip" -DestinationPath "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest" -Force

# Discover available platform/build-tools versions
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\cmdline-tools\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -First 10
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\cmdline-tools\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "build-tools;" | Select-Object -First 10

# Install required components (adjust versions from discovery above)
$sdkmgr = "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\cmdline-tools\bin\sdkmanager.bat"
& $sdkmgr "platform-tools" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "emulator"
echo y | & $sdkmgr --licenses
```

## 5. (Optional) Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& $sdkmgr --list | Select-String "system-images;android-"

# Install an image and create AVD
& $sdkmgr "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"
& "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\cmdline-tools\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"
```

## 6. Enable Windows Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Must run as Administrator
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## 7. Verify Everything

```powershell
# Check workloads
dotnet workload list

# Check Android SDK
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\platform-tools\adb.exe" version

# Check Java
java -version

# Create and build a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest -o "$env:TEMP\MauiTest"
dotnet build "$env:TEMP\MauiTest" -f net9.0-android
dotnet build "$env:TEMP\MauiTest" -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

---

**Key notes:**
- **Option A in step 4** is the simplest — run it inside any MAUI project and it auto-installs the correct Android SDK/NDK versions.
- Adjust `net9.0` to match your installed SDK version (`dotnet --version`).
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled in Windows Features for emulator hardware acceleration.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
